label: Photography
adaptation: Typography as the hero subject: large photoreal 3D letterforms with believable studio materials (matte paint, brushed metal, or frosted acrylic), crisp edge highlights from large softboxes, natural shadow falloff and contact shadows, specular response faithful to photography; integrate with the background via matching white balance and depth of field so the set reads as a real product shoot, not CGI plastic.

label: Teal & Orange
adaptation: Hollywood blockbuster grade: push teal into shadows and midtones, warm tungsten or sunset orange in skin- and highlight-adjacent regions, lifted black floor with filmic contrast; letterforms should show rim-lit edges, atmospheric haze interaction, and subtle halation—cohesive with a cinematic wide establishing background.

label: Glass
adaptation: Solid-glass letterforms: thin-shell refraction, dispersion fringes at glancing angles, internal bubbles or frost optional, caustic pools and sharp light shards cast onto surfaces behind; maintain energy-conserving speculars and parallax so typography feels volumetric and physically present in the scene.

label: Dreamcore
adaptation: Soft-liminal typography: slightly inflated or pillowy letter volumes, pastel bloom and gentle chromatic drift, low-contrast edges, subtle grain; letters may float with mild perspective skew but remain readable—match the surreal, comforting unease of dreamcore lighting (glow, fog, soft vignette).

label: Knit Fabric
adaptation: Knitted yarn letterforms: visible stitch rows, occasional dropped-stitch imperfections, fuzzy fiber scatter, warm subsurface softness; directional light rakes across loops to reveal micro-texture—typography should feel hand-knit and tactile, harmonized with a cozy craft-environment background.

label: Retro Film
adaptation: 35mm photochemical look: fine grain structure, halation on bright edges, slight gate weave, lifted shadows with milky blacks, occasional lens dust and edge light leak; color as faded Ektachrome or Kodachrome memory—typography inherits the same emulsion texture and period lens softness.

label: Wool Felt Style
adaptation: Dense wool-felt letters: fibrous fuzzy silhouette, pressed-layer edges, matte light absorption, tiny pilling; soft studio key with gentle bounce fill—forms read as handcrafted appliqué integrated into a felt-diorama scene.

label: Macaron Color
adaptation: Pastel patisserie palette: desaturated sweet hues with airy gradients, soft ambient occlusion in crevices, sugar-dust micro-highlight; glossy-macaron shell suggestion on rounded letter corners—overall dreamy, clean, and appetizing without neon saturation.

label: Plaster
adaptation: Cast plaster or stucco letter sculpture: chalky matte response, fine cracks and pitting, classical studio chiaroscuro; subtle ivory warmth and dust in recesses—typography should feel museum-statuary against a complementary classical or atelier background.

label: Plush Texture
adaptation: Velvet-plush letterforms: dense pile shading that shifts with light direction, visible seam lines and tufting, ultra-soft specular breakup; warm key light to emphasize tactile pile—readable silhouettes with cuddly material honesty.

label: Cartoon C4D
adaptation: 3D cartoon plastic: saturated candy colors, rounded bevels, exaggerated squash-friendly proportions, crisp contact shadows; Pixar-adjacent global illumination with bounce color bleed—typography matches a playful C4D world with clean rim lights.

label: CG Rendering
adaptation: High-end CGI hero pass: multi-bounce GI, accurate roughness/metalness, thin-film or anisotropic highlights where appropriate, subtle DOF; SSS on translucent materials—letterforms should read as award-show productviz quality aligned with a premium HDRI stage.

label: Steampunk
adaptation: Brass-and-copper mechanical letters: riveted plates, miniature gears, vented pistons, wisps of steam, oil sheen in crevices; warm tungsten key with cool fill bounce from steel—Victorian-industrial detail density without cluttering legibility.

label: Pixel Art
adaptation: Strict pixel grid typography: no sub-pixel anti-aliasing on letter edges, limited indexed palette, selective dither between ramps, crisp 1–2 px highlights; letters sit in the same perspective and lighting rules as an authentic retro game backdrop.

label: Ghibli
adaptation: Hand-painted anime backgrounds with soft watercolor skies and gentle foliage detail; typography as cel-painted shapes with mild outline, warm daylight wrap, no harsh speculars—whimsical, pastoral, emotionally bright, matching Ghibli-like environmental storytelling.

label: Anime Cartoon
adaptation: Modern TV-anime cel look: bold ink outlines with variable weight, flat fills plus hard-edged cel shadows, occasional gradient-only backgrounds; punchy saturated colors, screen-tone optional in shadows—dynamic readable letterforms suited to an urban or sky-action backdrop.

label: Impasto Oil
adaptation: Heavy impasto oil: thick palette-knife ridges casting micro-shadows, bristle track visibility, wet-looking peaks; color mixing on the surface—typography as sculpted paint mass consistent with a painter's studio environment.

label: Monet
adaptation: Impressionist broken color: small complementary brush dabs, vibrating light on edges, soft lost-and-found contours; atmospheric perspective with blue-violet distance—letterforms dissolve slightly into light like a Monet bridge or lily study.

label: Dunhuang Murals
adaptation: Mineral-pigment fresco look: aged mineral reds and greens, micro-crackle, gold leaf accents on halos or ribbons, dry-brush mineral texture; flat decorative perspective with flowing Dunhuang drapery motifs integrated around letterforms.

label: Design Draft
adaptation: Architectural pencil/technical draft: precise linework, light construction ticks, restrained graphite value range, trace-paper ghost lines; typography reads as measured blueprint lettering aligned to a drafting-table scene.

label: Watercolor Painting
adaptation: Transparent watercolor: wet-on-wet blooms, granulation in shadows, paper deckle awareness, soft backruns; cool-to-warm transitions—letters built from luminous washes rather than opaque fills, matching botanical or sky wash backgrounds.

label: Cyberpunk
adaptation: Neon glass typography: emissive edge tubing, holographic shimmer, rain streak refraction, wet-street bounce light; cyan/magenta split with deep shadow noise—letterforms feel integrated into a rainy megacity alley with volumetric fog.

label: Pencil Sketch
adaptation: Graphite rendering: HB to 6B range, directional hatching and cross-hatch for form, smudge in ambient occlusion, paper tooth visible; crisp letter contours with soft tonal modeling—consistent with observational still-life sketch lighting.

label: Chinese Ink Wash
adaptation: Sumi-e ink discipline: wet-to-dry brush pressure modulation, intentional white reserve, misty mountain dissolve at edges; restrained palette (ink + slight warm wash)—letterforms as brush constructions harmonized with negative space.

label: Surrealism
adaptation: Surrealist object-logic: partial melt, scale paradox, soft antigravity lift, painterly cohesion; muted surreal palette with one accent hue—typography participates in dream narrative without becoming illegible abstraction.

label: Art Nouveau
adaptation: Whiplash curves and floral ironwork integration: letters grown from stems and tendrils, stained-glass color patches, whiplash asymmetry; brass or enamel material hints with elegant filigree—typography as jewelry-like architecture.

label: Baroque Painting
adaptation: Baroque oil drama: strong tenebrism, glazes on deep reds and golds, ornate carved surrounds implied in light; letterforms modeled like marble or gilded stone under candle and window keys—grand, theatrical, readable silhouettes.

label: Gothic
adaptation: Victorian gothic letter architecture: pointed arch negative spaces, tracery, weathered stone and iron, moonlit rim with cold fill; desaturated palette with violet shadow—moody but sharp-edged readability.

label: Minimalist
adaptation: Swiss-modern restraint: generous whitespace, single sans family logic, micro-contrast through subtle shadow or paper emboss only; one restrained accent color or material truth—no ornament beyond precise grid alignment.

label: Abstract Expressionism
adaptation: Gestural energy fields: sweeping strokes, splatter accretion, impasto and thin paint in conflict; high color velocity around letterforms—typography emerges from abstract canvas chaos while preserving letter identity.

label: Ukiyo-e
adaptation: Woodblock print flat planes: notan separation, bokashi gradient edges, keyline contour dominance, limited flat pigments; stylized waves or Fuji distance as compositional rhyme—letterforms as printed shapes with carved edge honesty.

label: Vector Flat Illustration
adaptation: Vector-flat discipline: uniform stroke weights, snap-to-grid curves, solid fills, long-shadow or minimal isometric depth only; editorial clarity—typography matches contemporary app or magazine illustration systems.

label: Low Poly
adaptation: Faceted low-poly mesh letters: hard normals, triangulation visible, flat-shaded lambert with optional rim, crisp cast shadows; color per face or gentle vertex color—consistent with stylized game environment lighting.

label: Papercut Art
adaptation: Layered paper craft: stacked color sheets with contact shadow gaps, micro paper fiber edge, laser-cut precision; directional light to emphasize depth between layers—letterforms built from overlapping paper strata.

label: Embroidery Texture
adaptation: Machine or hand embroidery: satin stitch sheen, directional thread highlight, fabric weave underlay; thread color blocks with tidy edge density—typography stitched onto cloth consistent with hoop-and-textile scene.

label: Blueprint Sketch
adaptation: Cyanotype/blueprint line language: uniform technical lineweight, dimension tick marks, leader lines optional, white ink on deep blue field or inverse; engineering clarity—letterforms as stamped title block typography.

label: Film Noir
adaptation: Monochrome silver-gelatin: hard key from venetian blind or streetlamp, deep crushed blacks, high micro-contrast, slight halation; wet pavement reflections—letterforms sculpted by single-source noir lighting.

label: Vintage Polaroid
adaptation: Instant-film chemistry: lifted blacks, warm shift, soft lens glow, frame border with chemical pods suggestion, uneven development swirl; snapshot spontaneity—typography feels printed into a memory photo.

label: Pixar 3D Animation
adaptation: Feature-animation surfacing: rounded appealing forms, subsurface on skin-like plastics, warm bounce light, expressive but clean silhouettes; cinematic single-key hero light—letters as character-props in a Pixar-like story world.

label: Comic Book Style
adaptation: American comic printing: bold ink holds, flat CMYK fills, Ben-Day or halftone in shadows, spotted blacks; kinetic outlines and occasional speed lines—letterforms as cover-logotype energy matching four-color city action.
